---
title: 'Command-line interface'
---

```sh
syncweaver --help
```
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from ccbr_tools.shell import shell_run


def extract_commands(help_text: str) -> list[str]:
    """Extract command names from a Click-style help message.

    Args:
        help_text: Full help text emitted by a Click command.

    Returns:
        list[str]: Command names listed in the help text Commands section.
    """
    lines = help_text.splitlines()
    commands: list[str] = []
    in_commands = False
    done = False
    for line in lines:
        if not done:
            if not in_commands:
                if line.startswith("Commands:"):
                    in_commands = True
            elif not line.strip():
                if commands:
                    done = True
            elif not line.startswith(" "):
                done = True
            else:
                parts = line.strip().split()
                if parts:
                    commands.append(parts[0])
    return commands


def print_help_block(heading: str, help_text: str) -> None:
    """Print a markdown heading and fenced CLI help text block.

    Args:
        heading: Markdown heading to print before the code block.
        help_text: CLI help text to place in the fenced block.

    Returns:
        None: Always returns ``None``.
    """
    if heading:
        print(heading, end='\n\n')
    print("```")
    print(help_text)
    print("```", end='\n\n')
    return None


help_msg = shell_run("syncweaver --help")
print_help_block("", help_msg)

# get top-level commands from the help message
commands = extract_commands(help_msg)
for cmd in commands:
    cmd_help = shell_run(f"syncweaver {cmd} --help")
    print_help_block(f"### {cmd}", cmd_help)

    subcommands = extract_commands(cmd_help)
    for subcmd in subcommands:
        sub_help = shell_run(f"syncweaver {cmd} {subcmd} --help")
        print_help_block(f"#### {cmd} {subcmd}", sub_help)

```
